text.title: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) Procedure
text.summary: Step-by-step procedure to apply the arch-pattern-ddd methodology end-to-end.

step.name: event-storming
step.action: Run an event storming session: orange stickies = events, blue = commands, yellow = aggregates.

step.name: carve-contexts
step.action: Group events/aggregates by language boundary; name each cluster a bounded context.
step.output: Bounded context list with brief charters.
step.decision-gate: If fewer than 2 contexts identified, escalate — domain may be too simple for DDD.

step.name: define-aggregates
step.action: Pick aggregate roots; list invariants per aggregate; mark entities vs value objects.

step.name: catalogue-events
step.action: Per aggregate, list domain events (past tense) that change state.

step.name: draw-context-map
step.action: Document upstream/downstream + relationship pattern (open host, anti-corruption layer, conformist, customer-supplier).

step.name: ubiquitous-glossary
step.action: Build glossary with per-context owner; tag overloaded terms.
step.decision-gate: If a term has the same name in two contexts with different meanings, mark as overloaded and resolve naming.
